children.text: shall during the negotiation period with respect to such drug, in accordance with this section, negotiate a maximum fair price for such drug for the purpose described in ; andsection 1320f–2(a)(1) of this title

renegotiate, in accordance with the process specified pursuant to subsection (f), such maximum fair price for such drug for the purpose described in if such drug is a renegotiation-eligible drug under such subsection.section 1320f–2(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Methodology and process
children.label: 1 Methodology and process
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and use a consistent methodology and process, in accordance with paragraph (2), for negotiations under subsection (a) that aims to achieve the lowest maximum fair price for each selected drug.

children.heading: Submission of information
children.label: A Submission of information
children.text: Not later than March 1 of the year of the selected drug publication date, with respect to the selected drug, the manufacturer of the drug shall submit to the Secretary, in accordance with , the information described in such section.section 1320f–2(a)(4) of this title

children.heading: Initial offer by Secretary
children.label: B Initial offer by Secretary
children.text: Not later than the June 1 following the selected drug publication date, the Secretary shall provide the manufacturer of the selected drug with a written initial offer that contains the Secretary’s proposal for the maximum fair price of the drug and a concise justification based on the factors described in subsection (e) that were used in developing such offer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of receipt of an initial offer under subparagraph (B), the manufacturer shall either accept such offer or propose a counteroffer to such offer.

children.label: I
children.text: shall be in writing; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall be justified based on the factors described in subsection (e).

children.heading: Response to counteroffer
children.label: D Response to counteroffer
children.text: After receiving a counteroffer under subparagraph (C), the Secretary shall respond in writing to such counteroffer.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: E Deadline
children.text: All negotiations between the Secretary and the manufacturer of the selected drug shall end prior to the first day of November following the selected drug publication date, with respect to the initial price applicability year.

children.label: i
children.text: exceeds the ceiling determined under subsection (c) for the selected drug and year; or

children.label: ii
children.text: as applicable, is less than the floor determined under subsection (d) for the selected drug and year.

children.heading: Ceiling for maximum fair price
children.label: c Ceiling for maximum fair price

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The maximum fair price negotiated under this section for a selected drug, with respect to the first initial price applicability year of the price applicability period with respect to such drug, shall not exceed the lower of the amount under subparagraph (B) or the amount under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Covered part D drug
children.label: i Covered part D drug
children.text: In the case of a covered part D drug (as defined in ), the sum of the plan specific enrollment weighted amounts for each prescription drug plan or MA–PD plan (as determined under paragraph (2)).section 1395w–102(e) of this title

children.heading: Part B drug or biological
children.label: ii Part B drug or biological
children.text: In the case of a drug or biological product for which payment may be made under part B of subchapter XVIII, the payment amount under for the drug or biological product for the year prior to the year of the selected drug publication date with respect to the initial price applicability year for the drug or biological product.section 1395w–3a(b)(4) of this title

children.heading: Initial price applicability year 2026
children.label: i Initial price applicability year 2026
children.text: In the case of a selected drug with respect to which such initial price applicability year is 2026, the average non-Federal average manufacturer price for such drug for 2021 (or, in the case that there is not an average non-Federal average manufacturer price available for such drug for 2021, for the first full year following the market entry for such drug), increased by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (all items; United States city average) from September 2021 (or December of such first full year following the market entry), as applicable, to September of the year prior to the year of the selected drug publication date with respect to such initial price applicability year.

children.heading: Initial price applicability year 2027 and subsequent years
children.label: ii Initial price applicability year 2027 and subsequent years
I
children.text: the average non-Federal average manufacturer price for such drug for 2021 (or, in the case that there is not an average non-Federal average manufacturer price available for such drug for 2021, for the first full year following the market entry for such drug), increased by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (all items; United States city average) from September 2021 (or December of such first full year following the market entry), as applicable, to September of the year prior to the year of the selected drug publication date with respect to such initial price applicability year; or

children.label: II
children.text: the average non-Federal average manufacturer price for such drug for the year prior to the selected drug publication date with respect to such initial price applicability year.

children.label: A
children.text: the negotiated price of the drug under such plan under part D of subchapter XVIII, net of all price concessions received by such plan or pharmacy benefit managers on behalf of such plan, for the most recent year for which data is available; and

children.label: i
children.text: the numerator of which is the total number of individuals enrolled in such plan in such year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the denominator of which is the total number of individuals enrolled in a prescription drug plan or an MA–PD plan in such year.

children.heading: Short-monopoly drugs and vaccines
children.label: A Short-monopoly drugs and vaccines
children.text: With respect to a selected drug (other than an extended-monopoly drug and a long-monopoly drug), 75 percent.

children.heading: Extended-monopoly drugs
children.label: B Extended-monopoly drugs
children.text: With respect to an extended-monopoly drug, 65 percent.

children.heading: Long-monopoly drugs
children.label: C Long-monopoly drugs
children.text: With respect to a long-monopoly drug, 40 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In this part, subject to subparagraph (B), the term “extended-monopoly drug” means, with respect to an initial price applicability year, a selected drug for which at least 12 years, but fewer than 16 years, have elapsed since the date of approval of such drug under or since the date of licensure of such drug under , as applicable.section 355(c) of title 21section 262(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: A vaccine that is licensed under and marketed pursuant to such section.section 262 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: A selected drug for which a manufacturer had an agreement under this part with the Secretary with respect to an initial price applicability year that is before 2030.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: C Clarification
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (B)(ii) shall limit the transition of a selected drug described in paragraph (3)(A) to a long-monopoly drug if the selected drug meets the definition of a long-monopoly drug.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In this part, subject to subparagraph (B), the term “long-monopoly drug” means, with respect to an initial price applicability year, a selected drug for which at least 16 years have elapsed since the date of approval of such drug under or since the date of licensure of such drug under , as applicable.section 355(c) of title 21section 262(a) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: B Exclusion
children.text: The term “long-monopoly drug” shall not include a vaccine that is licensed under and marketed pursuant to such section.section 262 of this title

children.heading: Average non-Federal average manufacturer price
children.label: 6 Average non-Federal average manufacturer price
children.text: In this part, the term “average non-Federal average manufacturer price” means the average of the non-Federal average manufacturer price (as defined in ) for the 4 calendar quarters of the year involved.section 8126(h)(5) of title 38

children.heading: Temporary floor for small biotech drugs
children.label: d Temporary floor for small biotech drugs
children.text: In the case of a selected drug that is a qualifying single source drug described in and with respect to which the first initial price applicability year of the price applicability period with respect to such drug is 2029 or 2030, the maximum fair price negotiated under this section for such drug for such initial price applicability year may not be less than 66 percent of the average non-Federal average manufacturer price for such drug (as defined in subsection (c)(6)) for 2021 (or, in the case that there is not an average non-Federal average manufacturer price available for such drug for 2021, for the first full year following the market entry for such drug), increased by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (all items; United States city average) from September 2021 (or December of such first full year following the market entry), as applicable, to September of the year prior to the selected drug publication date with respect to the initial price applicability year.section 1320f–1(d)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Research and development costs of the manufacturer for the drug and the extent to which the manufacturer has recouped research and development costs.

children.label: B
children.text: Current unit costs of production and distribution of the drug.

children.label: C
children.text: Prior Federal financial support for novel therapeutic discovery and development with respect to the drug.

children.label: D
children.text: Data on pending and approved patent applications, exclusivities recognized by the Food and Drug Administration, and applications and approvals under or for the drug.section 355(c) of title 21section 262(a) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: Market data and revenue and sales volume data for the drug in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: The extent to which such drug represents a therapeutic advance as compared to existing therapeutic alternatives and the costs of such existing therapeutic alternatives.

children.label: B
children.text: Prescribing information approved by the Food and Drug Administration for such drug and therapeutic alternatives to such drug.

children.label: C
children.text: Comparative effectiveness of such drug and therapeutic alternatives to such drug, taking into consideration the effects of such drug and therapeutic alternatives to such drug on specific populations, such as individuals with disabilities, the elderly, the terminally ill, children, and other patient populations.

children.label: D
children.text: The extent to which such drug and therapeutic alternatives to such drug address unmet medical needs for a condition for which treatment or diagnosis is not addressed adequately by available therapy.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a renegotiation-eligible drug (as defined in paragraph (2)) that is selected under paragraph (3), the Secretary shall provide for a process of renegotiation (for years (beginning with 2028) during the price applicability period, with respect to such drug) of the maximum fair price for such drug consistent with paragraph (4).

children.heading: Addition of new indication
children.label: A Addition of new indication
children.text: A selected drug for which a new indication is added to the drug.

children.heading: Change of status to an extended-monopoly drug
children.label: B Change of status to an extended-monopoly drug
i
children.text: is not an extended-monopoly or a long-monopoly drug; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for which there is a change in status to that of an extended-monopoly drug.

children.heading: Change of status to a long-monopoly drug
children.label: C Change of status to a long-monopoly drug
i
children.text: is not a long-monopoly drug; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for which there is a change in status to that of a long-monopoly drug.

children.heading: Material changes
children.label: D Material changes
children.text: A selected drug for which the Secretary determines there has been a material change of any of the factors described in paragraph (1) or (2) of subsection (e).

children.heading: All extended-monopoly negotiation-eligible drugs
children.label: A All extended-monopoly negotiation-eligible drugs
children.text: The Secretary shall select all renegotiation-eligible drugs described in paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: All long-monopoly negotiation-eligible drugs
children.label: B All long-monopoly negotiation-eligible drugs
children.text: The Secretary shall select all renegotiation-eligible drugs described in paragraph (2)(C).

children.heading: Remaining drugs
children.label: C Remaining drugs
children.text: Among the remaining renegotiation-eligible drugs described in subparagraphs (A) and (D) of paragraph (2), the Secretary shall select renegotiation-eligible drugs for which the Secretary expects renegotiation is likely to result in a significant change in the maximum fair price otherwise negotiated.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall specify the process for renegotiation of maximum fair prices with the manufacturer of a renegotiation-eligible drug selected for renegotiation under this subsection.

children.heading: Consistent with negotiation process
children.label: B Consistent with negotiation process
children.text: The process specified under subparagraph (A) shall, to the extent practicable, be consistent with the methodology and process established under subsection (b) and in accordance with subsections (c), (d), and (e), and for purposes of applying subsections (c)(1)(A) and (d), the reference to the first initial price applicability year of the price applicability period with respect to such drug shall be treated as the first initial price applicability year of such period for which the maximum fair price established pursuant to such renegotiation applies, including for applying subsection (c)(3)(B) in the case of renegotiation-eligible drugs described in paragraph (3)(A) of this subsection and subsection (c)(3)(C) in the case of renegotiation-eligible drugs described in paragraph (3)(B) of this subsection.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: 5 Clarification
children.text: A renegotiation-eligible drug for which the Secretary makes a determination described in before or during the period of renegotiation shall not be subject to the renegotiation process under this section.section 1320f–1(c)(1) of this title

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: g Clarification
children.text: The maximum fair price for a selected drug described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (1) shall take effect no later than the first day of the first calendar quarter that begins after the date described in subparagraph (A) or (B), as applicable.12So in original. Probably means subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (1) of .1section 1320f–1(e) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be preceded by “such”.2
